changelog: Initial release: plugin loading, sidebar list, custom window design, settings persistence, auto-update
Add: applying downloaded updates (Updater.exe backs up the previous version, replaces host files, restarts the host)
Add: download/extract progress and error/retry states in the update overlay
Change: Updater.exe ships inside each release zip instead of the install folder, and deletes itself after applying an update
Fix: test release for verifying the update-installation flow end to end
Fix: an unhandled exception if Updater.exe failed to start (e.g. blocked by antivirus) silently crashed the host instead of showing an error
Add: show the reason in the title bar when the update check itself fails (e.g. GitHub API rate limit), instead of silently showing nothing
Fix: Updater.exe could fail silently while copying files (e.g. a file briefly locked after the host closed), leaving neither the old nor new version running
Add: Updater.exe now retries locked-file errors, restores the previous version from backup if the update itself fails, and writes progress to Release/Updater.log
Fix: a race between the host closing itself and Updater.exe starting up could leave neither the old nor new host running
Change: the host no longer closes itself after launching Updater.exe — Updater.exe now kills the host process by PID before replacing files

Fix: Updater.exe never received its arguments correctly because the install path ends in a backslash, which escapes the closing quote in the Windows command line and corrupts argument parsing — clicking Update would get stuck on "Installing update..." forever
Change: pass arguments to Updater.exe via ArgumentList instead of a manually-quoted string
Add: application icon ("Sn" on accent background) in the title bar, taskbar, and .exe
Add: custom application-wide font (Fontin Bold)
Add: live CPU/RAM usage of the SnHelper process on the Performance tab
Add: check for updates every 10 minutes while the app is running, not just at startup
Change: the released host is now a single SnHelper.exe (framework-dependent, .NET Desktop Runtime still required on the user's machine) instead of an exe with separate .dll/.runtimeconfig.json files
Add: landing page on GitHub Pages (SnHelper-releases), showing the current version and a download link, updated automatically on each publish
Change: update checks now read version.json on GitHub Pages instead of the GitHub Releases API, so they're no longer subject to the 60-requests-per-hour anonymous rate limit
Change: periodic update check interval reduced from 10 minutes to 20 seconds, now that it's no longer rate-limited
Add: update overlay now shows the changelog of every version between the currently installed one and the latest, not just the latest
Change: update button and open overlay now refresh live on every periodic check, instead of freezing on the version found when the overlay was opened
Change: update overlay changelog is now grouped by version with green version headers, gray text, and separators between versions
Add: the app now remembers the selected tab or plugin and restores it on the next launch
Fix: with no saved tab yet (first run), or a saved plugin that no longer loads, General is now selected instead of leaving the sidebar with nothing highlighted
Add: the taskbar icon now flashes when a new update is found, until the window is activated
Add: Performance tab now shows each loaded plugin's CPU load in milliseconds per tick, alongside host CPU/RAM
Change: plugin load on the Performance tab is now shown as a table with icon, name, and load columns, instead of plain text lines
Fix: added a visible separator and spacing between the process metrics and the plugin load table on the Performance tab
Add: plugins can now provide their own UI, shown in the work area instead of the description text
Add: each plugin can be individually enabled or disabled with a toggle switch above its view; disabled plugins stop ticking, turn gray in the sidebar, and move to the bottom of the plugin list
Add: TLI-Logs plugin, reading Torchlight Infinite's UE_game.log and showing new lines live in its own view
Fix: disabling a plugin could freeze the window for up to 3 seconds because all plugins shared one cancellation token, so Tick() never actually saw a stop request until the host itself closed

Change: the Performance tab is now shown as cards (host metrics, plugin load list) instead of plain text, with a relative load bar under each plugin's number, plugin rows spaced with no divider line between them
Change: removed the Developer tab for now
Fix: the plugin load status on the Performance tab said "остановлен" instead of "stopped" — the app's UI is English-only
Change: disabled plugins are no longer listed on the Performance tab, since they don't tick and have no load to show

Change: the plugin load bar on the Performance tab is now scaled against a fixed 1ms ceiling instead of the busiest plugin currently running, so it reflects actual load instead of always looking nearly full with a single plugin loaded

Change: redesigned the update changelog overlay — each version is now its own card, every changelog line has a small icon for Add/Fix/Change instead of the text prefix, the panel is capped at 80% of the main window's size with a themed thin scrollbar when it overflows
Fix: the update changelog scrollbar did not appear or scroll, because MaxHeight on the panel does not constrain a ScrollViewer nested inside a StackPanel
Fix: changelog lines were clipped instead of wrapping, because a horizontal StackPanel gives its child unlimited width so TextWrapping had nothing to wrap against
Change: the update overlay is now twice as wide (720px) and drops the redundant version header, since the version is already shown in the title bar button and each version card
Add: the update overlay now opens automatically once per newly found version, instead of requiring a manual click on the title bar button
Change: replaced the Fontin-Bold display font with JetBrains Mono across the whole app
Add: General tab now has an Appearance card to pick the app's font (JetBrains Mono, Inter, Fira Code, Roboto Mono) and text size, applied immediately without restarting
Add: Segoe UI (the Windows system font) to the font picker
Change: font files are now copied next to SnHelper.exe in a Fonts folder instead of being embedded in the assembly, so they don't bloat the single-file exe
Change: SnHelper.exe is now always published as a single file, even from a plain local build, not just from the release script
Change: publish-release.ps1 no longer asks for confirmation before publishing — it publishes automatically once the build succeeds
Change: Segoe UI is now the default font instead of JetBrains Mono
Change: default font size is now 12 instead of 13

Fix: the updater was carrying the old Plugins folder forward on every update instead of installing the new release's plugin builds, silently leaving users on stale/broken plugin versions after updating

Fix: an interrupted update (locked file, antivirus scan, disk issue) could leave the install folder with a mix of old and new files, or in some failure cases delete the old SnHelper.exe before the new one finished copying — requiring a full reinstall. The updater now stages the new install in a separate folder, verifies it's complete, and only then swaps it into place with an atomic rename; a failure at any point now leaves the previous working install untouched

Fix: publish-release.ps1 failed to parse with a "terminator expected" error on machines whose system codepage isn't UTF-8 (e.g. Cyrillic Windows-1251), because a non-ASCII character in the script was misread and broke string parsing for the rest of the file
Change: the font and font-size controls on the General tab now use a custom dark-themed look matching the rest of the app, instead of the default white Windows ComboBox/Slider

Change: rebuilt the update flow to remove the previous window where an interrupted update could leave the app broken. Updater.exe now launches immediately when Update is clicked and waits idly for the host to exit, instead of being launched at the last moment from a freshly downloaded and not-yet-verified archive

Change: the host downloads and extracts the update into a staging folder while it is still running, and only closes itself once the staged install is verified complete — a failed download or extraction now shows a retry option without ever touching the running install

Change: if the host closes without a completed update (e.g. it was closed manually while a download was in progress), the updater now relaunches the existing install automatically instead of leaving the app closed

Change: Updater.exe now ships inside every release install instead of only inside the update archive, and runs from a temporary copy while active so it never locks the install folder it needs to replace
Fix: the updater no longer shows a console window while running
Fix: testing

Change: Updater.exe is no longer a file that ships in the install folder next to SnHelper.exe. It's now embedded inside SnHelper.exe itself and extracted to a temp folder only for the moment an update runs, so a normal install never has an Updater.exe sitting on disk

Fix: the update flow's staging/backup folders and log file were accidentally being computed as a path inside the install folder instead of next to it, which would have made the swap step fail outright the first time this new update flow ran for real
Fix: testing2
Fix: testing3

Change: the updater no longer renames or removes the install folder as part of applying an update. It now only copies the new files on top of the existing ones (overwriting matching files), so the install folder itself is never moved, replaced, or briefly missing during an update
Fix: testing3

Fix: the previous release (1.1.20) shipped a broken build - the single-file publish step silently failed, producing a small framework-dependent SnHelper.exe with no bundled Fonts, which crashed immediately on startup with "SnHelper.dll not found"
Add: publish-release.ps1 now checks the built SnHelper.exe's size and confirms the Fonts folder exists before packaging a release, so a silently failed single-file publish can no longer be shipped
